name: Guadramilese

ancestors.notes: Medieval Asturian-Leonese of Portugal: the common ancestor of Guadramilese, Rionorese, and Mirandese.

Rionorese sister dialect. Guadramil and Rio de Onor are 5 km apart; the two varieties share ~85% of their phonological rules and diverge principally in morphological paradigms (article system, imperfect 3pl suffix, imperfect of ir) and in a handful of sibilant and vowel realizations.
Sanabrian Leonese (Zamora province), geographically adjacent across the border. Shared features include betacism and θ-merger.
Old Portuguese adstrate: intensifying contact pressure from the 15th century onward drove Portuguesification of the article system and lexical replacement.

notes: Guadramilese (guadramilés), the nearly-extinct Asturleonese variety spoken in Guadramil, municipality of Vinhais, Bragança district, Portugal — approximately 5 km south of Rio de Onor along the Portuguese-Spanish border. Fewer than 50 speakers as of the early 2000s. No dedicated linguistic monograph exists. Primary data source: Miro (2026) Paper III ('One Recording, Zero Grammar'), which analyses a ~400-word transcript of two elderly speakers describing traditional wedding customs. Supporting sources: Carvalho (1984) comparative notes; Santos (1967) 'falares fronteiriços de Trás-os-Montes'. PHONOLOGICAL PROFILE: Guadramilese is phonologically near-identical to Rionorese — all grapheme-to-IPA rules are inherited via graphemes_base from ast-PT-x-rionor. The shared Astur-Leonese phonological core includes: full betacism (v→b), Leonese e-diphthongization (e→ie), o-diphthongization (o→uo), e-syncope before s+C, imperfect -aba/-ában, ch→ʃ, tch→tʃ, x→ʃ, ç/z→θ, c+e/i→θ, g+e/i→x, positional rhotics (trill word-initial/post-C, flap elsewhere). DIVERGENCES FROM RIONORESE (morphological/lexical — not encodable in grapheme→IPA system, documented here for reference): (1) MASCULINE SINGULAR ARTICLE: Guadramilese uses Portuguese 'o' (not Rionorese/Leonese 'al'). Feminine 'la', masculine plural 'los', feminine plural 'las' remain Leonese — a frequency-driven partial paradigm replacement audio-confirmed in Paper III §5.1. Code-switching analysis rules out transcription artefact: INF2, the most dialectal speaker, consistently produces 'o noibo', 'o cura', 'o almuorço' (Portuguese article + maximally dialectal noun). This is the highest-confidence Guadramilese isogloss. (2) 3PL IMPERFECT 3RD CONJUGATION: '-ien' (dezien, fazien, salíen) vs. Rionorese '-ian'. 3 consistent attestations, no counter-examples. Aligns with central Asturian; Rionorese aligns with the western pattern. (3) IMPERFECT OF 'ir': 'diba/diban' (from Leonese infinitive stem 'dir-', regularized: dir→diba as tener→tenia). 6+ attestations, no counter-examples. Not documented in Rionorese; represents a morphological innovation (paradigm leveling of the suppletive ir/iba pair). (4) 1SG PRONOUN: 'you' [jow] vs. Rionorese 'ieu/iou' [jew]. Both Leonese forms (cf. Asturian 'yo', Mirandese 'you'). (5) PRODUCTIVE e-SYNCOPE: es+C→s+C extends productively beyond 'estar→star' to general position (smola < esmola). Rionorese treats only 'estar' as a lexical exception. (6) con-→cum- PREFIX RAISING: /o/→/u/ in unstressed prefix before voiced C (cumbidaba < convidava, cumbersar < conversar). 2 attestations; not confirmed in Rionorese. (7) SIBILANT REALIZATION (low-confidence, tentative): transcript 'diç' may indicate Guadramilese retains dental /θ/ or /s/ where Rionorese shifted to palatal /ʃ/ in the 'dizer' paradigm — but this may be a transcription artefact. Requires audio confirmation. Not encoded in this spec.

sources.author: Penny, R.
